text.title: Gce Autoscaling Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for the gce-autoscaling methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale + concrete remediation hook.

rule.statement: Default to CPU-target autoscaling at 60–70% utilisation for stateless compute-bound workloads.
rule.rationale: 60–70% leaves headroom for burst without keeping idle capacity expensive.

rule.statement: Worker MIGs processing a queue MUST scale on a Cloud Monitoring custom metric (queue depth or oldest message age), not on CPU.
rule.rationale: CPU is a lagging indicator for queue workers; scaling on queue depth keeps tail latency bounded.

rule.statement: Predictable daily/weekly patterns MUST use scaling schedules in addition to dynamic policies.
rule.rationale: Pure dynamic scaling lags morning ramp-ups; schedules pre-warm capacity.

rule.statement: Set `--min-num-replicas` ≥1 for services where cold start is user-visible; document why if 0.
rule.rationale: min=0 hits cold start every quiet period; explicit policy makes the trade-off auditable.

rule.statement: `--cool-down-period` MUST be ≥ instance boot + warmup time so the autoscaler doesn't double-scale.
rule.rationale: Too-short cooldown amplifies scale-out into oscillation and overshoots target.

rule.statement: If the input fails ALL "Applies If" preconditions or matches ANY "Skip If" disqualifier, do not apply this methodology — route the task to the adjacent sibling instead.
rule.rationale: Even flat checklists need an explicit skip leaf in the decision tree; this rule is the universal terminal.
